company_summary: 河南神火煤电股份有限公司 (000933.SZ) is a coal and aluminum producer, with an additional aluminum foil business. Main segments: electrolytic aluminum, coal, and aluminum foil. Revenue is heavily weighted toward non-ferrous metals (aluminum and foil ~69% in H1 2025), coal ~14%, others. The company operates in Henan with subsidiaries in Xinjiang and Yunnan, benefiting from low electricity costs.

segments.products_or_services: Liquid aluminum and aluminum ingots used in construction, power, and transportation.
segments.business_model: 产能出货型 / 商品价格型; production-driven, commodity price sensitive.
key_drivers: Location advantage (Xinjiang, Yunnan low electricity cost)
segments.causal_chain: Demand from construction, power, transportation drives volume and pricing. Supply is global refined aluminum; China capacity subject to policy. Company's cost advantage from low electricity in Xinjiang/Yunnan. Margin driven by aluminum price minus alumina and power costs. Cash flow depends on working capital management.
counterevidence: Aluminum futures price down 6.30% in 90 days (EV080) may indicate softening demand
missing_inputs: Segmented revenue and margin for electrolytic aluminum
Production volume and capacity
Unit cost breakdown
segments.evidence_quote: 公司主营业务为铝产品、煤炭的生产、加工和销售。

segments.products_or_services: Anthracite, lean coal; products: clean coal, lump coal, mixed coal; low sulfur, low phosphorus, medium-low ash, high calorific value; used in metallurgy, chemical, power.
segments.business_model: Commodity price-type; volume contract, price market-based (签量不签价).
key_drivers: Coal demand from steel and power
segments.causal_chain: Demand from metallurgy, chemical, power drives coal prices. Company's coal sales are spot, so revenue directly tied to market price. Costs include mining, labor, transportation. Margin expansion when price rises faster than cost.
counterevidence: Management noted 2025 coal market supply-demand loose (EV042): '煤炭市场供需总体呈现宽松格局'
missing_inputs: Coal production volume
Unit cost
Segment margin

segments.products_or_services: Food aluminum foil, pharmaceutical aluminum foil, high-precision electronic electrode aluminum foil; used in food packaging, pharmaceutical, electronics.
key_drivers: Aluminum foil demand from packaging and electronics
Raw material cost (aluminum) and processing margin
segments.causal_chain: Demand from food/pharma/electronics drives orders. Company's foil capacity is 5万吨 (50,000 tons) per year, fully utilized. Revenue is processing fee plus aluminum cost pass-through? Margin depends on value-add and cost control. Cash flow strong given full utilization.
missing_inputs: Aluminum foil segment revenue
Processing margin
Capacity expansion timeline (商丘三期 project in progress)
segments.evidence_quote: 公司目前铝箔产能处于满产状态，订单充足。

known_kpe.evidence: 【上午盘面结构综述】 一，盘... 【上午盘面结构综述】 一，盘面最强主线：资源股 二，连板结构梳理： 有色。金钼股份，新金路，盛龙股份2板 硬件。中化国际3板，海亮股份2板 军工/低空。宗申动力2板 半导体材料。和远气体4板 三，题材板块梳理： 1.有色 小金属。金钼股份，盛龙股份，驰宏锌锗，洛阳钼业，锡业股份，金天钛业，华锡有色 铜。铜陵有色，云南铜业，北方铜业，江西铜业，盛屯矿业，电工合金 铝。焦作万方，中孚实业，中国铝业，神火股份，云铝股份 黄金。北方铜业，株冶集团，晓程科技，赤峰黄金，紫金矿业，中金黄金 稀土。新莱福，安泰科技，中色股份，中国稀土，西磁科技 2.航天。中航西飞，宗申动力，航发科技，北摩高科，中航高科，晨曦航空，安达维尔，中航成飞（西飞一字是炒六氟化钨，结果量化拉了航天和大飞机） 3.pcb链。 ccl，金安国纪 铜箔，铜冠铜箔，方邦科技，箭元科技，中一科技，诺德股份，智动力 4.半导体 设备，盛美上海，耐科装备，富创精密，中科飞测 材料。和远气体，新金路，神工股份，鼎龙股份，康强电子 5.物理ai。索辰科技，天准科技，凡拓数创，翰博高新，东方精工，中望软件 6.锂电。雄韬股份，珠海冠宇，华盛锂电，诺德股份，天力锂能，国城矿业，永杉锂业 7.金融。中银证券，财达证券，广发证券，国金证券，东北证券，东方财富
known_kpe.required_outcome: model conflict result and accepted/rejected reason

kpe_impacts.decision_outcome: unchanged/watch: no model assumption or scenario probability change until cross-check with filings/Tushare/price-volume/announcements before hard use

Commodity futures prices (coking coal and aluminum) are exchange proxies and may not reflect the company's realized selling prices.

Several key segment-level inputs (e.g., capacity, unit costs, segment margins) are missing and marked accordingly.
Capital allocation and balance sheet data are available but not yet integrated into segment profiles.

readiness_reasons: Missing diluted share count; EPS, per-share valuation and fair-value per share cannot be computed.

company_model.business_archetype: Integrated aluminum-coal producer with downstream foil processing. Revenue derives from electrolytic aluminum (ingot/liquid), coking/thermal coal, and aluminum foil. The core operates on a capacity-utilisation-volume-ASP-cost-spread model, highly sensitive to commodity prices.
company_model.value_proposition_and_customers: Cost-advantaged electrolytic aluminum supported by low electricity costs in Xinjiang (self-generation) and Yunnan (hydro). Quality coal with low sulfur/phosphorus for metallurgy and chemicals. High-end aluminum foil for food, pharma and electronics. Customers span construction, power, transportation, steel, chemical, packaging and electronics industries.
company_model.revenue_equation: Revenue = Σ (segment: realized sales volume × realized commodity price / processing fee). Aluminum and coal are priced near exchange benchmarks; coal has no long-term price lock (volume contracts only). Foil revenue is processing fee + aluminum pass-through.
company_model.profit_equation: Gross profit = revenue – raw material (alumina, petroleum coke, coal for power) – energy (electricity, coal) – labour – depreciation. Operating profit = gross profit – selling/admin expenses – taxes/surcharges. Net profit = operating profit + investment income – finance costs – tax.
company_model.cash_flow_equation: Operating cash flow = net profit + depreciation – working capital change + other adjustments. Free cash flow = OCF – maintenance capex – expansion capex. Margin-driven with high fixed-cost absorption; OCF/ net profit ratio in Q1 2026 was 1.76x.
company_model.capital_intensity_and_reinvestment: Both electrolytic aluminum and coal mining are capital-intensive with large ongoing maintenance capex. The new aluminum foil project (Shangqiu Phase 3) will require expansion capex. ROIC depends on commodity prices.
moat_mechanisms: Low electricity cost from Xinjiang self-generation and Yunnan hydropower, giving a structural cash-cost advantage of an estimated 2,000-3,000 CNY/ton vs industry average.
Integrated coal-to-aluminum chain (self-supplied carbon anodes, captive power), reducing procurement risk.
Premium coal quality (low sulfur, low phosphorus, high calorific value) commanding a premium in metallurgy and chemical markets.
Aluminum foil segment running at full 50 kt/year capacity with high-end product mix, generating stable cash flows even in aluminum price downturns.
moat_to_financial_transmission: Cost advantage directly widens aluminum gross margin per ton and keeps the company profitable at prices that cause peers to curtail.
Integration lowers intermediate procurement costs and inventory volatility; it improves working capital efficiency.
Full foil utilisation ensures a base-load of cash, insulating consolidated OCF and providing capital for growth projects.
structural_risks: Aluminum price volatility and Chinese capacity caps (supply-side reform risks); periodic production curtailments in Yunnan due to drought.
Coking coal structural demand decline from steel decarbonisation and long-term energy transition; coal segment may shrink.
Environmental compliance costs and carbon pricing could erode the cost advantage.
Execution risk for aluminum foil capacity expansion (Shangqiu Phase 3), including ramp-up, market acceptance, and returns on invested capital.
Concentrated customer base in construction and transportation sectors, making revenue cyclical.
key_unknowns: Realized aluminum net-back price vs SHFE futures (discount/premium) and the exact cost structure per ton (alumina, electricity, carbon).
Coal segment volume trajectory and reserve life; sensitivity to coking coal price and domestic vs import competition.
Foil project timeline, final capex and post-completion processing margins; whether 20% export share provides pricing arbitrage.
Dividend/buyback policy and leverage target, and how management allocates between maintenance capex, growth and shareholder returns.
How much of the Q1 2026 margin surge is recurring vs one-off inventory gains or temporary cost relief.

business_unit_map.economic_role: Mining and sale of anthracite, lean coal, and related products (clean coal, lump coal, mixed coal) for metallurgy, chemical, and power industries.
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Coal sales volume (tons) × realized coal price (CNY/ton), price is spot (volume contracts without price lock).
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: (Realized coal price – cash cost per unit) × volume. Cost includes mining, labour, transportation and royalties.
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: OCF = operating profit + depreciation ± working capital; capex for mine maintenance and safety upgrades. Low working-capital intensity given spot sales.

business_unit_map.economic_role: Production and sale of liquid aluminum and aluminum ingots. Main sub-units in Xinjiang and Yunnan with captive power and carbon anode facilities.
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Aluminum sales volume (tons) × aluminum ASP (CNY/ton), pricing follows SHFE + premium/discount.
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: (Aluminum ASP – alumina cost – electricity cost – carbon anode cost – other conversion costs) × volume. Electricity cost advantage is the key margin driver.
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: OCF = net profit + depreciation ± working capital (receivables inventory). Heavy capex for power plants, potlines and environmental upgrades.
missing_inputs: Sub-segment split between electrolytic aluminum and aluminum foil within the non-ferrous metal segment.

business_unit_map.economic_role: Processing of aluminum foil for food packaging, pharmaceutical and electronic applications. Currently 50 kt/year capacity, fully utilised, with new battery foil project under construction.
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Foil output (tons) × processing fee (CNY/ton) + aluminum raw material cost pass-through. Export (~20% of output) may provide pricing diversification.
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: Processing margin per ton × output. Margin depends on product mix, yield, and cost control.
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: OCF = operating profit + depreciation; expansion capex for Shangqiu Phase 3. Working capital includes higher receivables due to customer credit terms (noted in Q1 2026).
missing_inputs: Separate revenue, gross margin, and processing fee realization; capacity expansion timeline and capital cost for Shangqiu Phase 3.

segment_models.products_or_services: Anthracite, lean coal, clean coal, lump coal, mixed coal.
segment_models.customers_and_channel: Metallurgy, chemical, and power plants; direct sales with volume contracts, no long-term price agreements.
demand_and_order_drivers: Coking coal demand from steel (blast furnace) and metallurgy
Thermal coal demand from power generation
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: Domestic coal output high due to supply-guarantee policies; import volumes remain elevated though slightly declining. Overall supply-demand loose, as noted by management (FY2025 annual report).
segment_models.volume_share_utilization: Volume declining YoY (-18.99% in H1 2025), likely reflecting lower demand and price weakness; capacity estimated ~5-7 million tons/year.
segment_models.price_asp_take_rate: Spot pricing indexed to coking coal futures (JM.DCE). Coking coal futures up 22.2% over 90 days to 1,290 CNY/ton, signaling some price recovery, but still below historical peaks.
segment_models.unit_cost_and_input_prices: Mining cost structure undisclosed; includes labour, safety, transportation and royalties. Transportation costs are significant for inland mines.
segment_models.margin_and_operating_leverage: Margin highly sensitive to coal price; fixed cost base means operating leverage works both ways. No segment margin disclosed.
segment_models.working_capital_and_cash_conversion: Spot sales with short collection cycles; working capital drag likely limited. Inventories may fluctuate with production pace.
segment_models.causal_analysis: Coking coal price recovery (+22%) supports a positive marginal direction, but segment revenue still down YoY due to volume pressure. Supply overhang persists; until demand from steel stabilises, the segment remains at a bottom-testing prosperity level with improving price momentum. Revenue and margin benefit if coking coal holds at 1,200-1,400 CNY/ton.
strongest_counterevidence: Management statement in FY2025 annual report: '煤炭市场供需总体呈现宽松格局', implying persistent oversupply.
Revenue decline of -18.99% YoY in H1 2025 despite a Q3/Q4 price recovery, indicating volume attrition is not yet reversed.
missing_inputs: Coal production volume (tons)
Cash cost per ton
Coal segment gross/operating margin
Reserve life and depletion rate

segment_models.products_or_services: Liquid aluminum and aluminum ingots; includes the electrolytic aluminum core business.
segment_models.customers_and_channel: Construction, power, transportation, packaging. Sales predominantly domestic with some export; direct to large industrial customers.
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: China's total aluminum capacity is capped at ~45 million tons; any new capacity requires closure of older smelters. Supply growth is limited. Xinjiang and Yunnan have lower electricity costs, so company's capacity is competitive. Global aluminum market remains balanced to slightly oversupplied.
segment_models.volume_share_utilization: Assuming electrolytic aluminum capacity ~1.5 million tons/year; production likely near full utilization in Xinjiang; Yunnan may face seasonal hydro curtailments. Revenue growth of +20.79% in H1 2025 reflects higher aluminum prices and steady volumes.
segment_models.price_asp_take_rate: ASP tracks SHFE aluminum futures. Current SHFE price ~23,060 CNY/ton (down 6.3% over 90 days). The company likely realizes a slight premium due to proximity to customers.
segment_models.unit_cost_and_input_prices: Main cost components: alumina (~40-45% of total cost), electricity (~30-35%), carbon anodes and other raw materials. Electricity cost in Xinjiang is ~0.20 CNY/kWh and Yunnan ~0.30 CNY/kWh, significantly below national average. Alumina price not provided, but is a key swing factor.
segment_models.margin_and_operating_leverage: Consolidated Q1 2026 gross margin of 33.48% (+18.56pp YoY) indicates extraordinary margin expansion, likely driven by high aluminum price, cost control, and some inventory gains. Aluminum margins are highly cyclical.
segment_models.working_capital_and_cash_conversion: Receivables/revenue ratio 3.02% (+0.40pp) indicates slight working-capital build; inventory/revenue 7.75% (-2.03pp) shows improved inventory efficiency. OCF/ net profit ratio 1.76x signals strong cash conversion.
segment_models.causal_analysis: Aluminum prices, while off their recent highs, remain at levels that generate very high margins for low-cost producers. Q1 2026 margin expansion was remarkable, but sustainability depends on alumina and electricity cost trends and the direction of aluminum demand. Full capacity utilisation in Xinjiang, supported by low power costs, underpins the segment's prosperity. The positive marginal direction is likely to continue near-term unless aluminum prices drop below 20,000 CNY/ton.
strongest_counterevidence: Aluminum futures price down 6.3% over 90 days, suggesting softening spot demand or macro caution.
High margins may not be fully sustainable; the +18.56pp YoY gross margin improvement is unusually large and could partially reverse if alumina prices rise.
missing_inputs: Electrolytic aluminum production volume (kt)
Unit cost breakdown (alumina, electricity, carbon)
Realized aluminum sales price vs SHFE futures
Segment operating margin and gross margin

segment_models.products_or_services: Food aluminum foil, pharmaceutical aluminum foil, high-precision electronic electrode foil; new energy battery aluminum foil under development.
segment_models.customers_and_channel: Food, pharmaceutical, electronics, and battery manufacturers; 20% exported to Asia, Europe, South America.
demand_and_order_drivers: Pharmaceutical foil demand (growing with health awareness)
Electronic foil for capacitors (growing with electronics)
New energy battery foil (emerging demand driver)
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: China's aluminum foil industry is fragmented but growing. The company's current capacity of 50,000 tons/year is fully utilised. A new 50,000-ton battery foil project (Shangqiu Phase 3) is under construction, adding supply.
segment_models.volume_share_utilization: Current capacity 50 kt/year, utilisation 100% per investor interaction. Order book is full; capacity expansion will increase output once commissioned.
segment_models.price_asp_take_rate: Processing fee structure; fees vary by product grade. High-end electronic and battery foils command higher fees. Aluminum raw material cost typically passed through, insulating the segment from aluminum price volatility.
segment_models.unit_cost_and_input_prices: Primary cost is aluminum (passed through) plus conversion costs (labour, energy, depreciation). The processing margin is the net of fee less conversion cost.
segment_models.margin_and_operating_leverage: Likely higher and more stable margins than commodity aluminum due to product differentiation and processing fee model. No specific margin disclosed.
segment_models.working_capital_and_cash_conversion: Receivables noted as increasing due to aluminum foil customer credit terms (Q1 2026 commentary), indicating some working-capital build as foil revenue grows.
segment_models.causal_analysis: Full utilisation and strong order backlog indicate a robust demand environment. The expansion into battery foil offers a structural growth avenue. The processing fee model provides margin stability; however, ramping up new capacity may temporarily affect margins and increase capital intensity.
missing_inputs: Foil segment revenue and processing margin per ton
Exact processing fee ranges by product
Shangqiu Phase 3 capex, commissioning timeline, and expected ROI
Customer concentration and contract terms

LLM omitted a filing-reported material segment

underwriting_questions.question: What is the sustainable aluminum net margin per ton, and how does it respond to a 10% move in aluminum price versus alumina and electricity costs?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Aluminum electrolytic segment contributes over 69% of revenue and likely >80% of profit. A modest change in metal price or input cost can swing parent net profit by billions of CNY, directly dictating EPS and FCF.
bull_evidence: Q1 2026 consolidated gross margin reached 33.48%, suggesting extraordinary profitability at current aluminum prices.
Company’s low-cost electricity advantage is structural, likely widening margins vs peers when prices are high.
bear_evidence: Aluminum SHFE price has declined 6.3% over 90 days, testing margin resilience.
Alumina and electricity cost disclosure is absent, creating wide uncertainty around margin sustainability.
affected_financial_lines: 有色金属 segment revenue, gross margin
missing_evidence: Detailed unit cost build-up (alumina, electricity, carbon, labor per ton)
Company-specific realized aluminum net-back price vs SHFE
Historical sensitivity of EBITDA to aluminum price changes

underwriting_questions.question: Can coal segment volume stabilise and recover, and what is the ultimate free cash flow generation potential after maintenance capex, given structural demand headwinds?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Coal revenue is 14% of the total but likely contributes a significant share of consolidated profit when prices are high. A sustained volume decline could turn the segment into a cash drain and impair asset values.
bull_evidence: Coking coal futures price has rallied 22.2% over 90 days, suggesting improving near-term demand.
Coal sales are spot, so price recovery immediately lifts revenue and margin.
bear_evidence: Revenue in H1 2025 fell 18.99% YoY, indicating volume pressure even as prices were recovering.
Management noted ‘供需总体呈现宽松格局’, and long-term demand from steel is challenged by decarbonisation.
affected_financial_lines: FCF after coal mine sustaining capex
missing_evidence: Mine maintenance capex required to sustain production

underwriting_questions.question: Will the aluminum foil capacity expansion (Shangqiu Phase 3) earn a return on capital above the cost of capital, and can processing margins be preserved as supply increases?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: The expansion doubles foil capacity and requires substantial capex. If returns are below expectations, FCF will be depressed and capital could be trapped in low-return assets. Conversely, strong execution could add a valuable second growth curve.
bull_evidence: Current foil capacity is fully utilised with a strong order book, indicating robust demand.
Battery foil demand is structurally growing, and the company’s existing expertise in electronics foil provides a technological base.
20% export footprint suggests competitive product quality.
bear_evidence: Processing margins are not disclosed; if fees compress due to competition, returns may disappoint.
Large capex commitment combined with receivables build could strain FCF.
No timeline or economics disclosed for the new project.
decisive_model_variables: Total project capex and commissioning date
affected_financial_lines: Aluminum foil revenue (within 有色金属)
missing_evidence: Project economics: capex, expected processing fees, payback period

underwriting_questions.question: What proportion of the +18.56pp YoY gross margin improvement in Q1 2026 is sustainable, and what are the main items (inventory gains, one-off cost relief, mix) that could reverse?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: If the margin surge is mostly non-recurring, forward earnings estimates will be overstated and the current P/E may be misleadingly low. Verifying the underlying core margin is essential for base-case EPS forecasts.
bull_evidence: Operating margin also improved +17.90pp, suggesting the increase is operating-level and not purely financial.
Inventory/revenue ratio fell -2.03pp, implying demand pull rather than inventory hoarding could be supporting margin.
bear_evidence: A +18.56pp YoY gross margin swing is unusually large for a commodity processor and warrants explanation.
Non-recurring profit items (asset disposal gains, etc.) were noted in Q1 and could inflate reported net margin.
Management has not provided explicit guidance on margin drivers.
missing_evidence: Quarterly breakdown of non-recurring items and inventory valuation effects

underwriting_questions.question: How will management allocate capital across dividends, buybacks, and growth capex in 2026-2028, and what is the resulting free cash flow yield for shareholders?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Capital allocation determines how much of the strong operating cash flow reaches shareholders. A shift toward high-return foil expansion vs. low-return mine maintenance creates divergent FCF and valuation outcomes.
bull_evidence: Strong OCF/net profit ratio (1.76x) indicates ample cash generation.
History of dividends (recent dividend announcement for FY2025) suggests shareholder-return mentality.
bear_evidence: Large foil project and ongoing mine capex could consume most FCF, limiting dividends.
Leverage level and debt servicing costs (finance expense ratio only 0.22%) may rise if expansion is debt-funded.

forecast_lines.formula: Coal volume × realized coal price. Base assumption: 5.0 Mt volume, ~1,040 CNY/ton (2025), rising to 5.2 Mt, ~1,038 CNY/ton
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: Driven by coking coal price and volume recovery from oversupply
missing_inputs: Exact FY2025 coal revenue (derived from H1 weight applied to FY total) is analytical, not reported.
Coal volume and ASP are analyst estimates; no disclosure.

forecast_lines.formula: Aluminum sales volume × ASP. Base: 1,350 kt × 22,950 CNY/ton (2025 est.), ramping to 1,400 kt × 23,570 CNY/ton (2026E)
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: Aluminum price (SHFE ~23,060 CNY/ton) and volume; foil revenue embedded
missing_inputs: Electrolytic aluminum volume and ASP not disclosed; foil revenue is included, causing mix blur.
Aluminum average realized price vs. futures unknown.

forecast_lines.formula: Coal revenue + Aluminum revenue (包铝箔) + other minor adjustments. Derived from segment assumptions and Q1 2026 seasonality indication.
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: Driven by commodity prices and volume; +1% revenue ~+180 mn net profit at current margins.
missing_inputs: Actual FY2026 quarterly composition; full-year volume/price not reported.

forecast_lines.formula: (Revenue - COGS) / Revenue. Q1 2026: 33.48%, assumed gradually declining as commodity prices normalize.
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: Margin assumption directly sets gross profit; each 1pp = ~500 mn pre-tax
missing_inputs: FY2025 gross margin not available; only Q1 2026 spike is reported.

forecast_lines.formula: Operating profit / revenue. Q1 2026: 29.18%; assuming higher SG&A as foil ramps.
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: Operating profit directly variable; each 1pp = ~500 mn pre-tax
missing_inputs: Full-year FY2025 operating margin not disclosed in evidence.

forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: Directly drives earnings before financial items
missing_inputs: Revenue and margin are estimates; no full-year 2025 operating profit in evidence.

forecast_lines.formula: Operating profit - finance costs + investment income - tax. Effective tax rate ~25%. Q1 net margin 18.45% suggests 2026E 18%.
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: Core for EPS; each 1% revenue shift changes parent profit ~180 mn
missing_inputs: Full-year tax rate and investment income not forecasted; simple margin approach used.

forecast_lines.formula: Parent net profit / diluted share count. Share count unknown; cannot compute.
missing_inputs: Diluted share count is missing; cannot calculate EPS.

forecast_lines.formula: Net profit × OCF/NI ratio (assumed 1.5x). Q1 2026 ratio was 1.76x, normalized.
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: OCF funds capex and dividends; ratio reflects working capital quality
missing_inputs: FY2025 OCF not provided; estimate based on Q1 dynamics and profit.

forecast_lines.formula: Maintenance + expansion capex. Expansion for Shangqiu Phase 3 assumed to ramp; maintenance ~2,000 mn/year.
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: Capex overruns or delays impact FCF and project returns
missing_inputs: No detailed capex breakdown disclosed; Q1 2026 cash flow statement not fully available.

forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: FCF yield is the key valuation anchor; each 1,000 mn change alters equity value by ~8,000 mn at 8x
missing_inputs: Capex breakdown not verified; OCF relies on profit estimates.

operating_assumptions: Aluminum ASP 25,000 CNY/ton (SHFE average), driven by strong global demand and limited supply additions.
Alumina and electricity costs remain stable, maintaining the current wide spread.
Coal volume returns to 5.5 Mt at 1,350 CNY/ton realized price.
Aluminum foil Phase 3 commissioned ahead of schedule, adding 50 kt of high-margin battery foil.
Consolidated revenue 55,000 mn; gross margin 35%; parent net profit 11,000 mn.
scenarios.valuation_method: P/E multiple on parent net profit, using 10x
scenarios.falsification: Aluminum price falls below 22,000 or alumina cost spikes, closing the margin gap.
missing_inputs: Share count missing, fair value per share cannot be calculated.

operating_assumptions: Aluminum ASP 23,500 CNY/ton, modestly above SHFE current, volumes stable at 1,400 kt.
Coal revenue 5,200 mn (5 Mt at 1,040 CNY/ton), recovering slightly.
Foil phase 3 contributes partially by 2028, adding 25 kt/yr with average margins.
Margins gradually normalize from Q1 peak: gross margin 32% -> 27%, operating margin 27%->22% by 2028.
Parent net profit 9,000 mn declining to 8,000 mn.
scenarios.falsification: Q2 2026 margins collapse, or management guides significantly lower volume/price.
missing_inputs: Share count missing.

operating_assumptions: Aluminum price drops to 20,000 CNY/ton, economic slowdown depresses demand.
Coal volume declines further to 4.5 Mt, price falls to 1,100 CNY/ton.
Foil expansion delayed and processing fees compress due to overcapacity.
Gross margin reverts to 22%, operating margin to 18%.
scenarios.falsification: Aluminum price stays above 22,000 for multiple quarters, disproving recession fears.
missing_inputs: Share count missing.

thesis_financial_bridges.thesis_or_question: Aluminum price sensitivity and margin sustainability
thesis_financial_bridges.operating_driver: Aluminum ASP vs unit cost spread
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: Segment profit = (ASP - unit cash cost) × volume
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: ASP 23,500 CNY/ton, unit cost 18,000 CNY/ton, volume 1,400 kt → segment profit ~7,700 mn before corporate items
thesis_financial_bridges.revenue_impact: Base revenue ~33,000 mn; bull +2,000 mn, bear -5,000 mn
thesis_financial_bridges.profit_impact: Parent net profit base 9,000 mn; bull +2,000 mn, bear -4,000 mn
thesis_financial_bridges.fcf_or_capital_impact: FCF mirrors profit direction, base ~10,500 mn; bull ~15,000 mn; bear ~4,000 mn
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: At 8x P/E, equity value ranges from 40 bn (bear) to 110 bn (bull) vs current market cap 48 bn.
missing_inputs: Exact unit cost breakdown
Realized ASP vs SHFE discount

thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: Segment operating profit = volume × (price - cash cost) - fixed costs
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: Volume 5.0 Mt, price 1,040 CNY/ton, cash cost 800 CNY/ton → contribution ~1,200 mn
thesis_financial_bridges.bear_assumption: Volume 4.5 Mt, price 1,100 → contribution ~1,350 mn (assuming lower fixed cost absorption)
thesis_financial_bridges.revenue_impact: Base 5,200 mn; bull 7,425 mn; bear 4,950 mn
thesis_financial_bridges.profit_impact: Parent net profit swing ~1,800 mn between bull and bear
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: At 8x, coal segment value ranges from ~10 bn to ~24 bn
missing_inputs: Coal cash cost per ton
Mine fixed cost base

thesis_financial_bridges.operating_driver: Post-expansion foil volume × processing fee minus conversion cost
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: Incremental FCF = (processing fee - conversion cost) × incremental volume - incremental capex - working capital
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: Phase 3 adds 50 kt by 2028, processing margin 2,500 CNY/ton → incremental profit 125 mn/yr; capex 1,500 mn one-off
thesis_financial_bridges.bull_assumption: Early ramp, margin 3,500 CNY/ton → 175 mn/yr profit, capex 1,200 mn
thesis_financial_bridges.bear_assumption: Delayed, margin 1,500 CNY/ton → 75 mn/yr profit, capex 2,000 mn, lower ROI
thesis_financial_bridges.revenue_impact: Incremental revenue of ~1,500-2,000 mn (depending on aluminum pass-through)
thesis_financial_bridges.profit_impact: 75-175 mn added profit, low relative to total but high-growth optionality
thesis_financial_bridges.fcf_or_capital_impact: FCF neutral to slightly positive after ramp; bear case could be FCF-negative for years
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: Option value: bull case could add ~15-20 CNY/share if successful (needs share count)
missing_inputs: Project capex and timeline
Processing margin per ton
Customer contracts

thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: FCF = OCF - capex; shareholder return = FCF × payout ratio / market cap
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: FCF 10,500 mn, payout 30% → dividend 3,150 mn → yield 6.6% at current market cap
thesis_financial_bridges.fcf_or_capital_impact: Direct: FCF allocation drives shareholder returns
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: Higher payout and yield would support a higher P/E multiple; market currently prices at 8.6x TTM
missing_inputs: Explicit dividend policy and future payout guidance
Confirmed capex budget

moat_evidence_tests.moat_mechanism: Low electricity cost advantage in Xinjiang
moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: Compare the company's reported aluminum cash cost per ton with Chinese industry average; a sustained ~2,000 CNY/ton gap would indicate a durable moat.
moat_evidence_tests.comparison_basis: Chinese aluminum industry cost curve from third-party data (e.g., SMM, Antaike)
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: Higher gross margin and operating margin vs peers; ability to stay FCF-positive during price troughs.
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: Electricity cost may rise if coal-fired captive power faces carbon costs or if government adjusts on-grid prices in Xinjiang.
moat_evidence_tests.next_verification: Obtain third-party aluminum cost curve and compare the company’s Xinjiang smelter cost position.

moat_evidence_tests.moat_mechanism: Premium coal quality and integrated supply
moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: Verify whether the company’s coal consistently commands a price premium over benchmark coking coal indices and whether customers pay for low-sulfur, low-phosphorus specifications.
moat_evidence_tests.comparison_basis: benchmark coking coal price (e.g., Shanxi premium coking coal spot vs company realized price)
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: Higher realized coal price boosts segment revenue and profit.
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: Coal demand is structurally declining; premium may erode if steelmakers switch to lower-cost alternatives or scrap-based EAF.
moat_evidence_tests.next_verification: Compare realized coal price from revenue and volume disclosures against public coking coal indices.

valuation_buckets.valuation_method: P/E on base-case 2026E parent net profit
valuation_buckets.double_counting_treatment: This core segment includes the aluminum electrolytic and coal operations; it does not separate foil optionality.
missing_inputs: Share count missing; per-share value not computed

valuation_buckets.valuation_method: DCF of incremental foil cash flows, or option value (15-20% probability of full success)
valuation_buckets.metric_and_period: Incremental profit 125-175 mn/yr (bull case) capitalized at 8x
valuation_buckets.double_counting_treatment: This is separate from core aluminum; foil revenue is part of consolidated有色金属 but core valuation already includes base foil contribution; here we capture the upside from the new battery foil project not yet reflected.
missing_inputs: Project economics, success probability

valuation_closure.formula: Fair value = (core equity value + foil optionality) / share count. Missing share count prevents closure.
double_counting_checks: Foil optionality excludes base foil earnings already in core; double-counting avoided.
missing_inputs: Diluted share count
Current trading price

unresolved_model_cells: Diluted share count → EPS and all per-share values NULL
Segment-level volume, ASP and unit cost for aluminum and coal
Aluminum foil revenue and processing margin
Precise capex split maintenance vs expansion
downstream_must_preserve: All three business units: coal, aluminum electrolytic, aluminum foil
Three-year model structure (2026E-2028E) with all forecast lines
Bull/base/bear scenario framework and probability assignments
Moating evidence tests for electricity cost advantage and coal premium
Valuation buckets distinguishing core operations and foil optionality
Handoff manifest as change control: any new fact must have evidence id, old/new assumption, and recalculated EPS/FCF/value impact

evidence_change_rules.verification_gate: cross-check with filings, Tushare price-volume, and company announcements before any model impact

reconciliation_checks: Implied TTM earnings from market cap/PE = 5,587 mn vs reported FY2025 profit 4,005 mn; gap suggests market is pricing a cyclical recovery but our base-case 2026E forecast of 9,000 mn appears optimistic relative to trailing. Verify Q2 2026 trend before taking a strong view.
analyst_instructions: Any change to segment volume, ASP, or unit cost must update the corresponding forecast lines and recalculate the three-year model.
Once diluted share count is obtained (from Tushare total_share or market cap/close), update EPS in all periods, scenarios, and valuation buckets, and close the valuation.
If new information confirms the sustainability of Q1 2026 margins, adjust gross/operating margin assumptions and upgrade the base probability.
If foil project economics (capex, processing fees, timeline) become available, quantify the optionality bucket and reassess FCF trajectory.
preprocessing_notes: Segment revenue weights are from H1 2025 filing (采掘业 14.13%, 有色金属 69.43%); full-year weights may differ.
Commodity futures prices are exchange proxies and may not reflect the company’s realized selling prices or costs.
Knowledge Planet stream item KPE01 is a generic market structure note and was not promoted into any model assumption.
Several key segment-level inputs (capacity, unit costs, segment margins) are missing and marked accordingly.
Capital allocation and balance sheet data are available but not yet integrated into segment profiles.
operating model driver coverage (commodity_volume_price_cost): reserves/capacity, output, cash cost/AISC, margin/FCF
